subtasks.description: Evaluate the bulk PII export request against data governance and compliance policies. Determine if the analyst has legitimate authorization for customer PII export, and assess whether the use case (analysis) requires additional safeguards, anonymization, or denial based on access control rules.

plan.cost_rationale: This is a governance decision that MUST precede any data export. A policy_action subtask is required because the query explicitly requests PII bulk export, which involves compliance, access control, and data protection policy evaluation. Mid tier is assigned because the decision requires multi-signal reasoning: evaluating the analyst's role/permissions, the legitimacy of the use case (general "analysis" is vague), applicable regulations (GDPR, CCPA, etc.), and organizational data governance policies. This is a single policy evaluation step—no SQL or extraction happens until policy clearance is obtained.

output.reason: Bulk PII export request cannot proceed. POL-501 requires explicit Legal and CISO approval which has not been obtained. POL-502 mandates data minimization; exporting names, emails, and phone numbers (all sensitive PII) requires documented business-critical justification. POL-503 can be satisfied with audit logging and retention controls, but is conditional on approval of other policies.
applied_constraints: Action denied pending Legal department approval
If approved: implement audit logging of all access and export events
If approved: enforce 30-day retention limit with automatic purge
Recommend: assess whether analysis can proceed with anonymized or minimized dataset instead of full PII export
Recommend: document specific business-critical justification for bulk PII access
